FlasherApplication.sourceSignature: class FlasherApplication { get criticalSection(): ApplicationActivity['criticalSection']; get admission(): ApplicationActivity['admission']; get activeOperation(): ApplicationOperation | undefined; initialize(): Promise<ApplicationSnapshot>; snapshot(): ApplicationSnapshot; scanDevices(): Promise<ApplicationActionResult>; connectDevice(candidate: PortCandidate): Promise<ApplicationActionResult>; disconnectDevice(): Promise<ApplicationActionResult>; recoverDevice(): Promise<ApplicationActionResult>; selectOemTarget(): Promise<ApplicationActionResult>; selectLocalFirmwareTarget(): Promise<ApplicationActionResult>; download(): Promise<ApplicationActionResult>; prepare(input: FirmwareUpdatePreflight): Promise<ApplicationActionResult>; detectDfu(): Promise<ApplicationActionResult>; refreshPrerequisites(): Promise<ApplicationActionResult>; flash(preparationId: string): Promise<ApplicationActionResult>; requestShutdown(): Promise<'safe' | 'blocked-critical'>; releaseForWindowClose(): Promise<'safe' | 'blocked-critical'>; resumeAfterWindowOpen(): void }

ApplicationDevicePort.sourceSignature: export interface ApplicationDevicePort { listDevices(): Promise<PortCandidate[]>; snapshot(): DeviceSnapshot; connect(candidate: PortCandidate): Promise<DeviceSnapshot>; disconnect(): Promise<void>; recoverAfterManualPowerOff(confirmation: typeof MANUAL_POWER_OFF_CONFIRMATION): Promise<DeviceSnapshot>; }

ApplicationUpdaterPort.sourceSignature: export interface ApplicationUpdaterPort { state(): Promise<FirmwareUpdateState>; snapshot(): FirmwareUpdateState; download(): Promise<FirmwareUpdateState>; prepare(input: FirmwareUpdatePreflight): Promise<FirmwareUpdateState>; detectDfu(): Promise<FirmwareUpdateState>; refreshPrerequisites(): Promise<FirmwareUpdateState>; flash(input: FirmwareFlashRequest): Promise<FirmwareUpdateState>; selectOemTarget(): Promise<FirmwareUpdateState>; admitLocalCustomTarget(target: LocalCustomFirmwareTarget, artifact: AdmittedFirmwareArtifact): Promise<FirmwareUpdateState>; }

NativeSafetyPromptPort.sourceSignature: export interface NativeSafetyPromptPort { confirmFirmwareWrite(input: FirmwareWritePrompt): Promise<boolean>; confirmPhysicalPowerOff(): Promise<boolean>; }

LocalFirmwareTargetSelection.sourceSignature: export interface LocalFirmwareTargetSelection { readonly target: LocalCustomFirmwareTarget; readonly artifact: AdmittedFirmwareArtifact; }

LocalFirmwareTargetPickerPort.sourceSignature: export interface LocalFirmwareTargetPickerPort { selectLocalFirmwareTarget(): Promise<LocalFirmwareTargetSelection | undefined>; }

FirmwareWritePrompt.sourceSignature: export type FirmwareWritePrompt = { readonly preparationId: string; readonly targetId: string; readonly targetVersion: string; readonly targetSha256: string; } & ({ readonly targetKind: 'oem'; } | { readonly targetKind: 'local-custom'; readonly targetManifestSha256: string; });

ApplicationTimeAndIdentityPort.sourceSignature: export interface ApplicationTimeAndIdentityPort { now(): Date; randomUuid(): string; }

invocations.outputDescription: Zero or more normalized current USB candidates.
invariants: The application revalidates every candidate before publishing it.

invocations.inputDescription: A validated target with its bound admitted artifact capability.
invocations.outputDescription: Verified custom-target state, or rebound awaiting-DFU state for a recovered preparation.

errors.description: The artifact capability does not reproduce the target digest, size, and manifest proof.

invariants: AdmittedFirmwareArtifact is target-ID-bound and openVerified binds reverified bytes to the exact descriptor inherited by dfu-util.
Rebinding a recovered prepared custom target clears stale DFU identity and requires fresh detection before flash.
invocations.validationBoundary: src/application/flasher-application.ts#ApplicationUpdaterPort.admitLocalCustomTarget owns the exact declared call boundary; referenced runtime validators apply before untrusted values are used.

invocations.inputDescription: Target identity shown through NativeSafetyPromptPort in a native main-process prompt; custom prompts include the full manifest SHA-256.
invocations.outputDescription: Operator decision supplied only to application policy.
invariants: The renderer never supplies a flash confirmation literal.
Custom confirmation binds artifact target ID and manifest SHA-256 so alternate provenance/qualification manifests cannot be conflated.
